examples: Zeaxanthin is a natural carotenoid existing in corn embryosperm.
Broccoli contains lutein and zeaxanthin (phytonutrient antioxidants in the carotenoid family).
A half cup of cooked Swiss chard provides a huge amount of both lutein and zeaxanthin, supplying 10 mg each.
Dr. Chew said the AREDS study researchers recommend replacing the beta-carotene in supplements with lutein and zeaxanthin.
The study found a similar beneficial effect for people with cataracts who don't normally get a lot of lutein and zeaxanthin in their diet.
